config/                      :

resources/                   :
  langs/
    en/                      : Language file for \`en\` locale
      phrase.php             : Define phrases for group \`phrase\`
      validator.php          : Define phrases for group \`validator\`
  menu/                      :
    menus.php                :

  routes/                    :
     api.php                 : Define RESTful API routes

  src/
    Contracts/               : Contains basic interface defination
    Database/                :
      Factories              : Contain database model [factories](https://laravel.com/docs/9.x/database-testing#defining-model-factories)
      Migrations             : Contain database [migrations](https://laravel.com/docs/9.x/migrations#main-content)
      Seeders                : Contain database [seeders](https://laravel.com/docs/9.x/seeding#introduction)
        PackageSeeder.php    : Entry point for database seeder

`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{

    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{

    <p><strong parentName="p">{`name`}</strong>{`: The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`name`}</inlineCode>{` of the app package. It consists of vendor name and project name, separated by /. For examples:`}</p>

    <p>{`The name must be lowercase and consist of words separated by -, . or `}<em parentName="p">{`. The complete name should match ^`}{`[a-z0-9]`}{`([`}</em>{`.-]?`}{`[a-z0-9]`}{`+)`}<em parentName="p">{`/`}{`[a-z0-9]`}{`(<([`}</em>{`.]?|-{0,2})`}{`[a-z0-9]`}{`+>)`}{`_`}{`\\$.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`description`}</strong>{`: A short description of the app package. Shoule be one-line message.`}</p>

    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`authors`}</strong>{`: Info of the authors of the package. You can specify multiple `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`author`}</inlineCode>{` objects here. An `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`author`}</inlineCode>{` object can have following properties:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`name`}</inlineCode>{`: The author's name. Usually their real name.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`email`}</inlineCode>{`: The author's email address.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`homepage`}</inlineCode>{`: URL to the author's website.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`require`}</strong>{`: Map of packages required by this package. The app package will not be installed unless all requirements are met.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`require-dev`}</strong>{`: Map of packages required for developing this package, or running tests, etc. The dev requirements of the root package are installed by default. Both `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`install`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`update`}</inlineCode>{` commands support the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`--no-dev`}</inlineCode>{` option to prevent dev dependencies from being installed.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`autoload.psr-4`}</strong></p>
    <p>{`Under the psr-4 key you can define a mapping from namespaces to paths relative to the package root. When autoloading a class like `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Foo\\\\Bar\\\\Baz`}</inlineCode>{`, and a namespace prefix `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Foo\\\\`}</inlineCode>{` are pointed to a directory `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`src/`}</inlineCode>{`, the autoloader will look for a file named `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`src/Bar/Baz.php`}</inlineCode>{` and include it if existing. Note that as opposed to the older PSR-0 style, the prefix `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`(Foo\\\\)`}</inlineCode>{` is not present in the file path.`}</p>
    <p>{`Namespace prefixes must end in `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`\\\\`}</inlineCode>{` to avoid conflicts between similar prefixes. For example: Foo would match classes in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`FooBar`}</inlineCode>{` namespace. Thus, we use the trailing backslashes to solve the problem: Foo`}{`\\`}{` and FooBar`}{`\\`}{` are distinct.`}</p>
    <p>{`The PSR-4 references are all combined, during installation or update, into a single key => value array which may be found in the generated file `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`vendor/composer/autoload_psr4.php`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`autoload.files`}</strong></p>
    <p>{`If you want to require certain files explicitly on every request then you can use the file autoloading mechanism. This is very useful if your app package need to include PHP functions that cannot be autoloaded by PHP`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{

    <p><strong parentName="p">{`autoload-dev`}</strong></p>
    <p>{`This section allows to define autoload rules for development purposes.`}</p>
    <p>{`Classes needed to run for testing purposes only should not be included in the main autoload rules to avoid polluting the autoloader in production and when others use your app package as a dependency.`}</p>
    <p>{`Therefore, it is a good idea to rely on a dedicated path for your unittests and add it within the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`autoload-dev`}</inlineCode>{` section.`}</p>

    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{

    <p><strong parentName="p">{`extra.metafox.namespace`}</strong>{`: The root namespace of the package. It must use trailing backslashes with "`}{`\\`}{`". For example: `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`MetaFox\\\\Blog`}</inlineCode></p>
